subject: Project Management
title: The Manhattan Project
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Manhattan Project.

hook.text: In 1942, the U.S. launched a secret project to build an atomic bomb before Nazi Germany.

buildup.text: General Groves managed 125,000 people across 30 sites. Most didn't know what they built.

discovery.text: Oppenheimer led the science at Los Alamos while Groves handled logistics and secrecy.

twist.text: The project cost $2 billion — about $30 billion today — and was done in just three years.

climax.text: On July 16, 1945, the first nuclear bomb detonated. Oppenheimer quoted Hindu scripture.

punchline.text: The most consequential project was also the most secret.

quiz.question: How many people worked on the Manhattan Project?
